subject: process_model
what: no persistent CLI processes; each turn is a fresh shell-out
why: 2026-04-05 spike showed all three CLIs expose non-interactive JSONL mode with session resumption; pty/tmux was dead code

subject: concurrency
what: one in-flight turn per conversation, FIFO queue
why: CLIs are single-threaded interactive processes; principle #1 seam requires serialization above the adapter

subject: filesystem
what: each CLI turn runs in an ephemeral scratch cwd; no access to user files
why: OpenAI chat API has no cwd concept; only coherent interpretation is to suppress CLI native file tools at the sandbox

subject: claude_exit_code
text: claude -p exits 0 even when a rate_limit_event with status != allowed was emitted. Never infer health from exit code; always inspect the JSONL event stream for rate_limit_event and result.subtype.

subject: cold_cache_tax
text: Cold CLI invocation eats 6k (gemini) to 14k (codex) input tokens of agent-prompt overhead before the user's prompt is even processed. claude's prompt-cache window is 1h. If jRouter idles >1h, first turn back pays the full tax. Warm-cache discipline is a routing concern, not just an optimization.

subject: agent_contamination
text: Under OAuth subscription auth, the backend CLIs run as tool-wielding agents framed by their own system prompts. A trivial prompt ('reply ok') produced num_turns:3 in the spike — internal agent work the client didn't request. This is observable (log num_turns and tool events per turn) but not preventable. Design for it, don't fight it.

subject: gemini_compound
text: Gemini auto-routes across sub-models within a single turn (spike saw gemini-3-flash-preview and gemini-2.5-flash-lite in the same call). stats.models has the per-model breakdown. GeminiAdapter must surface this as a compound-provider quota event, not a single counter.

subject: session_id_shapes
text: Three vendors, three session id shapes: claude accepts a client-chosen UUID via --session-id; gemini returns a server-assigned index in its init event; codex returns a server-assigned thread_id in thread.started. The adapter boundary must normalize this — the router stores an opaque string and never parses it.

goal: scaffold the orientation loop (STATUS, JOURNAL, CLAUDE.md, scripts/)

text: canonical project name is FreelOAder (package: freeloader); old 'jRouter'/'jrouter' references in non-journal files have been renamed
subject: project_name
why: repo dir, PLAN.md, README.md all use freeloader; the jrouter references in STATUS/ROADMAP/AGENT/CLAUDE/scripts were drift from a prior name and would break every gate on the first commit that creates src/

text: CLIAdapter.send() Protocol carries system: str | None = None from day one
subject: system_prompt_slot
why: phase-5 tool-call shim requires adapter-owned system-prompt slot (PLAN.md § 'Things to explicitly *not* do'); retrofitting the signature after ClaudeAdapter/GeminiAdapter/CodexAdapter exist is a three-way rewrite plus three contract-test rebaselines

text: chat-completions history diff lives in src/freeloader/canonical/history_diff.py — not the frontend, not the adapter
subject: history_diff_home
why: principle #6 says handlers are ~50 lines; decision #3 says adapters are text-in/text-out. Client edits/regenerations/reordered system messages need a real home. Canonical layer is the only coherent owner
source: PLAN.md principle #4, decision recorded in phase-1 exit criteria

text: Anthropic ToS crackdown (Jan 9, 2026 server-side block; Feb 19 policy; Apr 4 OpenClaw ban) prohibits OAuth tokens from Free/Pro/Max accounts being used in 'any other product, tool, or service' including the Agent SDK. Enforcement was server-side client-identity validation rejecting spoofed Claude Code headers — the OpenClaw-style pattern of extracting the OAuth token and calling api.anthropic.com directly. BUT Anthropic staff clarified that running the genuine claude binary as a subprocess is still allowed ('even on a remote server, even over SSH'); CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN is officially documented for CI/scripts. FreelOAder's MVP path (shell-out to claude -p --output-format stream-json) is the sanctioned pattern, not the banned one. --bare OAuth path is dead (confirmed). Remaining risks: (a) the written ToS is stricter than the staff clarification; (b) commercial/shared use requires API keys; (c) cold-cache tax >1h is the behavioral signature Anthropic flags wrappers by — warm-cache discipline per session_id is a ToS concern, not just cost.
subject: anthropic_3p_ban

text: phase 1 step 1.0 scaffolding complete — all loop artifacts + scripts committed in 8e01c36..17dd2dd; stale 'first commit' checkbox in prior STATUS was an artifact of the cold-start procedure

insert step 1.0.5 (architecture review) between 1.0 and 1.1 — adversarial review of PLAN.md + ROADMAP.md before first code lands; chosen as a step rather than a full phase to avoid re-litigating settled ground and to match the loop shape (AGENT.md § scope discipline)
subject: architecture_review_step
why: PLAN.md encodes 8 principles + 11 decisions + spike findings; after step 1.1 those harden into code. Cost of revising a PLAN.md line now is an edit; cost after src/freeloader/adapters/claude.py exists is a refactor plus test rebaseline. One-shot review step with JOURNAL entries as output — no new gate script, no new phase.

text: architecture review — adversarial pass over PLAN.md + ROADMAP.md before first code lands
Delta is a tagged union (TextDelta | SessionIdDelta | UsageDelta | RateLimitDelta); frontend pattern-matches per variant
subject: delta_tagged_union
why: flat Delta with 'last one has session id' semantics conflates content stream and control channel. Every adapter interleaves text, session id, usage, and rate-limit events on a single JSONL stream; variants must be distinguishable at every yield. PLAN.md principle #1 revised.

text: drop 'logged' as a separate terminal state; journal write is atomic with reaching a terminal state (complete / cancelled / backend_error / rate_limited / timed_out)
subject: turn_state_simplified
why: a 'drained but not yet logged' window creates a consistency gap — if the process crashes there, conversation history and JOURNAL disagree on the outcome. Journal-write failures surface as adapter_error. PLAN.md principle #2 revised.

text: history_diff MVP scope frozen at three cases (append, last-assistant regeneration, mismatch→raise); mid-history edits raise 400
subject: history_diff_mvp_scope
why: the fourth case (truncate stored history at divergence, replay from N onward) is cheap to add once the three-case spine works. Pre-building it is scope creep per AGENT.md § scope discipline. Revisit in phase 5 if a real client surfaces the need.

text: two logs, one format: global JOURNAL.jsonl for all events (build-time + per-turn + quota signals); per-conversation <data_dir>/<conversation_id>.jsonl for canonical messages only (replay on rebind). Router reads JOURNAL.
subject: storage_layout
why: principle #5 says quota is an event stream the router consumes; scanning N per-conversation files per routing decision doesn't scale. Separating events (global) from messages (per-conversation) matches the two access patterns. PLAN.md decision #6 revised.

text: canonical replay into a new backend contains only user messages and final-assistant-message turns; intermediate agent contamination preserved in metadata but not replayed
subject: replay_scope
why: OpenAI chat surface only exposes user + assistant roles. claude's num_turns>1 from a single client turn (internal tool work) was never part of the conversation the client thinks it's having. Lossy for the backend reasoning trace; faithful to the client-visible conversation. PLAN.md principle #3 revised.

text: no health() on CLIAdapter Protocol in MVP; phase 4 may add probe_quota() only if routing needs a decision before the first turn fires
subject: health_probe_deferred
why: principle #5 says quota is an event stream, not a probe. health() with ambiguous QuotaState return was conflating liveness (the subprocess) with quota (the subscription). Subprocess liveness is measured per-turn (did it spawn, did it exit with result). Remove now; retrofit cost after phase-1 code lands is one-time. PLAN.md principle #1 revised.

text: HTTP framework: FastAPI (async-native, first-class SSE via StreamingResponse, built-in OpenAPI docs, Pydantic request validation matches OpenAI JSON Schema error shapes)
subject: http_framework
why: previously only shown in README architecture diagram, never formalized as a decision — gap would let step 1.1 accidentally pick Starlette/Hypercorn. Every OpenAI-compatible proxy example uses FastAPI; client error shapes stay familiar. New PLAN.md decision #12.

text: runtime logging = stdlib logging; architectural events = JOURNAL.jsonl
subject: runtime_logging
why: two audiences, two destinations. stdlib logging (default WARNING, INFO with FREELOADER_LOG_LEVEL=info) for server-runtime diagnostics (port binding, spawn failures, stacktraces, request IDs). JOURNAL.jsonl for per-turn records + quota + decisions + phase transitions. Different retention, volume, consumers. No structlog/loguru — stdlib can emit JSON via formatter. New PLAN.md decision #13.

text: add three scope excluders: no static type checker in gate, no CI/pre-commit, no pytest plugins beyond pytest itself (pytest-asyncio likely exception, decide at step 1.1)
subject: scope_excluders_added
why: personal-use prototype ~500 lines; mypy/pyright fights Protocol + asyncio + duck-typed adapters for little gain until phase 3. GitHub Actions is overhead the MVP never pays back. Pytest plugins are the usual scope-creep vector. PLAN.md § 'Things to explicitly not do' updated.

text: concurrent JOURNAL.jsonl appends within the server process need a single-writer asyncio lock — POSIX O_APPEND is atomic only under PIPE_BUF (~4KB) and a long assistant message in a turn_done record can exceed that. scripts/reflect.sh is single-process so unaffected. Becomes relevant in phase 3+ when multiple turns run concurrently across conversations.
subject: journal_append_atomicity

text: claude's rate_limit_event has no counterpart 'allowed again' event — once status != 'allowed' the router switches and never switches back without explicit re-enable. resetsAt (unix timestamp) is the signal; phase 4 routing needs a scheduled re-enable (e.g., an asyncio.call_later firing at resetsAt or a polled probe). Without this, the first rate limit makes a backend permanently unreachable for the process lifetime.
subject: rate_limit_stickiness

text: architecture review complete — 13 findings, 7 PLAN.md revisions, 9 decisions + 2 lessons in journal, 1 deferred (/codex consult to step 1.1/1.2)
